description: Adversarial: a Class A artifact declaring a weaker hash algorithm ('SHA-1') than the protocol-frozen algorithm ('SHA-256') MUST receive INVALID. The v0.1.0 freeze pins the algorithm to SHA-256.

input.claim: this artifact matches its declared origin digest under a downgraded hash algorithm

why: WiseOrder Protocol v0.1.0 §4 and SPEC_LOCK_v0.1.md §2.1 freeze the hash algorithm to SHA-256. A Class A artifact declaring algorithm: 'SHA-1' — even if expected_digest matches observed_digest under SHA-1 — MUST be rejected as INVALID. Mitigates threat A-2 (algorithm downgrade): an attacker who can convince a verifier to accept a weaker algorithm can construct collisions and forge VERIFIED status. Exercises CS1 (algorithm declaration must match the protocol-frozen algorithm) and the v0.1.0 freeze rule. The digest format ('sha1:' prefix) further indicates non-conformance with the v0.1.0 'sha256:' digest format.
